title: StudyDataset.get_subject_data should reject boolean subject_id as positional index
description: Boolean subject_id values were being interpreted as integer indices after numeric fallback. R treats subject_id logical as invalid. Python should reject booleans and raise ConfigError.
notes: Fix in python/src/fmridataset/study_dataset.py: guard bool before numeric positional fallback; add regression test test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_disallows_boolean_subject_id. Command: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py
close_reason: Adjusted get_subject_data numeric fallback to ignore booleans so logical subject_id values no longer resolve as 1/2 positional IDs. Added regression test test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_disallows_boolean_subject_id and verified full access test file: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py

title: Expose latent component metadata fields in Python get_metadata
description: R latent backend metadata returns basis_variance, loadings_norm, loadings_sparsity in backend_get_metadata(); Python LatentBackend.get_metadata() was missing these keys. Add fields for parity and validate via latent dataset component-info tests.
close_reason: Aligned python/src/fmridataset/backends/latent_backend.py metadata payload with R parity fields (basis_variance, loadings_norm, loadings_sparsity, plus existing format/storage keys). Added regression assertions in python/tests/test_latent_dataset.py::test_get_component_info_metadata_fields. Verified via python -m pytest python/tests/test_latent_dataset.py -k get_component_info and full python test suite.

title: Align python latent_dataset get_mask with R component-mask semantics
description: R latent_dataset.get_mask returns n_components logical mask; python currently inherits backend voxel-mask. Update LatentDataset.get_mask and add regression.
close_reason: get_mask now returns component-level logical mask matching R semantics; regression test added.

title: fmri_series StudyDataset temporal_info should include subject_id
description: Implemented StudyDataset-aware temporal_info with run->subject mapping and added regression for subject_id alignment.
close_reason: Added StudyDataset-specific temporal_info builder (subject_id/run_id alignment) and regression in test_fmri_series_query.py; fmri_series() behavior now matches R metadata for study datasets.

title: LatentBackend should require open before data access
description: LatentBackend was raising ValueError when data methods used before open; added open guard with BackendIOError and added 2 regressions verifying get_data/reconstruct_voxels require open() first. Verified python -m pytest python/tests/test_latent_backend.py and mixed latent tests.
close_reason: LatentBackend now has _require_open() guards in get_data/reconstruct_voxels and tests already validate BackendIOError on closed access; full latent backend tests pass.

title: Add latent_dataset.reconstruct_voxels parity API in Python
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase B2: Shared template support for latent BIDS H5 mode
description: When all scans use the same fmrilatent spatial template (parcel_basis_template or build_hierarchical_template), store template once in /latent_meta/template/ and per-scan data as [T,K] projection coefficients only (no per-scan loadings). Depends on Phase B. Acceptance: compress_bids_study(mode='latent', template=my_template) uses shared template; per-scan data is only coefficients; file size significantly smaller; reconstruction works via template loadings x coefficients + offset.

title: Validate run_length strictly in latent_dataset
description: latent_dataset currently casts run_length with int(), allowing silent truncation of non-integer inputs unlike other constructors; add strict integer validation and regression.
close_reason: Rejects non-integer run_length in latent_dataset using shared _coerce_run_length validation and adds regression test.

title: Fix run_length truncation in constructors
description: Python _coerce_run_length in dataset_constructors already enforces integer run_length values via float.is_integer() and raises ValueError('run_length values must be integers'). Existing regressions in python/tests/test_dataset.py cover non-integer paths.
close_reason: Rejects non-integer run_length before sum validation and adds regression tests for matrix_dataset + fmri_dataset.

title: python latent metadata parity missing component statistics
description: Python latent backend metadata is missing R-parity fields exposed by get_component_info (basis_variance, loadings_norm, loadings_sparsity).
notes: Fixed: LatentBackend.get_metadata now mirrors R latent backend component stats with keys basis_variance, loadings_norm, loadings_sparsity; sparse-aware sparsity fallback added. Added assertions in python/tests/test_latent_backend.py for these fields.
close_reason: Closed by latent metadata parity fix in Python port; fields now included and regression coverage added.

title: Support file:// URIs in zarr_backend
description: Implemented zarr backend file:// URI support by extending remote-source detection in backend_open.zarr_backend. Added regression test in tests/testthat/test_zarr_backend.R. Regression test pass verified: test_zarr_backend.R and test_zarr_dataset_constructor.R with Rscript test_file(...).

title: latent_dataset get_data_matrix should avoid warning
description: Implemented fix: LatentDataset now defines get_data_matrix() as an alias for get_latent_scores(), matching R behavior. Added regression test test_get_data_matrix_no_warning in python/tests/test_latent_dataset.py. Test passes: PYTHONPATH=python/src pytest -q python/tests/test_latent_dataset.py (26 passed).
close_reason: Added LatentDataset.get_data_matrix override to match R behavior and regression test; latent dataset tests pass.

title: Normalize empty event_table in Python constructor
description: Python matrix/FmriDataset constructors now normalize zero-column/None event_table inputs to event_index-only frame for R parity; added regression tests in python/tests/test_dataset.py (default_event_table_has_event_index, empty_event_table_is_normalized).
close_reason: Implemented parity-compatible default empty event_table normalization in matrix_dataset/FmriDataset and added/validated regression tests in python/tests/test_dataset.py.

title: Phase A: Make latent_backend work with fmrilatent LatentNeuroVec without fmristore
description: latent_backend currently requires fmristore unconditionally (latent_backend.R:115). When source is a list of in-memory fmrilatent::LatentNeuroVec objects, fmristore should not be required. Also, slot access (obj@basis, obj@loadings) must handle fmrilatent's BasisHandle/LoadingsHandle lazy types via materialization helpers. Add fmrilatent to Suggests. Acceptance: latent_dataset(source=list(fmrilatent_lvec), TR=2, run_length=100) works without fmristore; get_latent_scores/get_spatial_loadings/reconstruct_voxels all work; validate_backend passes; fmristore .lv.h5 path workflow unbroken; R CMD check passes.

title: Latent closed-backend methods should fail
description: Added open-state checks for latent metadata and loadings: backend get_metadata now requires open; new get_loadings method with open validation; latent_dataset get_spatial_loadings now delegates to backend. Added regressions for closed-backend get_component_info, get_spatial_loadings, backend get_metadata, backend get_loadings raising BackendIOError. Verified with pytest on test_latent_backend and test_latent_dataset: 44 passed.

title: Phase B: Add latent compression mode to BIDS H5 archive
description: Extend compress_bids_study() and bids_h5_dataset() to support compression_mode='latent'. Writer uses fmrilatent::encode() per scan, storing basis [T,K], loadings [V,K], offset [V] in /scans/<name>/data/. Backend dispatches on compression_mode: latent reads basis as [T,K] (same contract as parcellated). Add get_loadings(), reconstruct_voxels(), encoding_info() methods on bids_h5_study_dataset. Depends on Phase A. Acceptance: compress_bids_study(mode='latent', encoding=spec_time_dct(k=15)) produces valid H5; get_data_matrix returns [T_total,K]; subset_bids_h5 works; reconstruct_voxels works; round-trip matches direct encode(); parcellated mode unaffected; R CMD check passes.

title: StudyDataset.get_subject_data should accept integer-valued float indices
description: R subject_id handling accepts numeric values like 1.0 as subject positions. Python previously rejected non-int scalars even when integer-valued. Fix numeric fallback to accept integer-valued floats and reject non-integer floats.
notes: Changes in python/src/fmridataset/study_dataset.py (numeric fallback guards), regressions in python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_numeric_float_index and ::test_get_subject_data_numeric_non_integer_index. Verified via: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py
close_reason: Updated StudyDataset.get_subject_data subject-id numeric fallback to accept integer-valued float subject identifiers/indices (e.g., 1.0) and reject non-integer numeric values (e.g., 1.5). Added regression tests test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_numeric_float_index and ::test_get_subject_data_numeric_non_integer_index. Verified with full file: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py

title: Add fmri_zarr_dataset parity constructor name in Python
description: R exposes fmri_zarr_dataset() constructor, but Python currently exports only zarr_dataset(), creating API/feature mismatch in cross-language usage and docs parity. Add an alias and regression.

title: Mirror fmri_study_dataset run_id injection/warning in Python study_dataset
description: Python study_dataset was missing R parity behavior: fmri_study_dataset adds run_id when absent and warns when dataset has multiple runs. Added warning + run_id injection for each subject plus regression in test_study_dataset.
close_reason: Implemented run_id parity in study_dataset: adds subject_id/run_id (with warning) when run_id missing, matching R behavior; added regression test validating inferred run_id and warning.

title: Temp test
close_reason: Closed

title: matrix_dataset should reject/flatten non-2D datamat like R as.matrix
description: Updated matrix_dataset datamat coercion: non-2D arrays now flatten with order='F' to a (n,1) matrix, matching R as.matrix behavior for >2D inputs. Added regression test test_multi_dimensional_datamat_flattens_like_r_as_matrix.
close_reason: Fixed matrix_dataset to reshape non-2D datamat inputs via np.reshape(-1,1,order='F') before validation, matching R as.matrix semantics; added regression in python/tests/test_dataset.py (test_multi_dimensional_datamat_flattens_like_r_as_matrix), pytest passes (19 passed).

title: Align matrix_dataset default event_table with R parity
description: matrix_dataset currently injected synthetic event_index column when event_table is empty, but R constructor keeps event tables empty. This diverges from parity and leaks non-empty metadata in normal usage.
close_reason: Aligned matrix_dataset default event_table behavior with R by preserving empty table and added tests; regression passes.

title: Fix latent backend get_mask open-state check
description: Parity bug: latent backend get_mask returns stale data after close because it only checks mask cache, unlike R backend_get_mask which errors when closed.
close_reason: Added open-state guard for latent backend get_mask and regression tests for pre-open and post-close access

title: Reset latent backend cached arrays before reopen
description: Reopen currently fails: after close, second open sees stale _loadings/_offset state and `open()` never sets n_voxels, raising BackendIOError. R backend supports reopen from closed state.
close_reason: Reset stale cached fields on reopen so latent backend can open again after close; added test_reopen_after_close regression.

title: latent_dataset run_length must sum to latent total time
description: Python latent_dataset constructor accepted explicit run_length values that did not sum to backend total time. R behavior validates this with an assertion before building sampling frame; port did not, causing invalid dataset shapes to be accepted. Added ValueError check in latent_dataset() and regression test.
close_reason: Added explicit run_length sum validation in latent_dataset and regression test; test file passes.

title: Align matrix_dataset default event_table behavior with R parity
description: Port diverged from R: matrix_dataset() was injecting synthetic event_index rows when event_table was None/empty; updated constructor to preserve empty event tables and pass through provided tables unchanged except for existing uniqueness validation.
close_reason: Fixed matrix_dataset parity: removed synthetic event_table injection and ensured event table is unchanged unless provided; added regression assertions for empty-default and explicit-empty cases; test_dataset passes.

title: LatentBackend get_data should validate row/col indices
description: Python LatentBackend accepted invalid row/column selectors (negative, out-of-range, non-integer float) without error; R backend enforces index bounds before subsetting. Added explicit validation for rows/cols and regression tests in python/tests/test_latent_backend.py.
close_reason: Implemented index validation in LatentBackend get_data (rows/cols bounds + integer checks) and added regression tests; pytest: 16 passed.

title: Support legacy array selectors in fmri_series resolve_selector
description: R parity allows raw integer coordinate matrices and logical selectors in resolve_selector; Python previously only accepted selector classes/1-D int arrays. Implemented legacy array handling (3-col 1-based coordinates, masked/full-volume boolean masks) and added regression tests for coord/mask selectors and output validation in test_fmri_series_query.py.
close_reason: Implemented legacy selector handling for fmri_series resolve_selector (3-col integer coordinate matrices and logical selectors for masked/full-volume masks), preserving class-based selectors; added regressions in test_fmri_series_query and verified full test suite passes.

title: Add latent_dataset.get_component_info parity to Python
close_reason: Closed

title: Make fmri_series coordinate selector out-of-bounds tolerant
description: R-style coordinate matrix selectors silently drop out-of-bounds rows; Python resolver raised errors and could index out-of-range in searchsorted path. Adjusted coordinate selector mapping to ignore out-of-range entries safely.
close_reason: Allowed coordinate selectors with out-of-bounds rows to be ignored (as R does), removed strict 1-based range checks and fixed searchsorted-boundary filtering to avoid OOB indexing; added regression test ; full python test suite passes.

title: StudyDataset.get_subject_data should handle empty subject list
description: Calling get_subject_data with subject_id=[] currently crashes with IndexError. It should return an empty matrix instead.
notes: Fix in python/src/fmridataset/study_dataset.py and add regression test test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_empty_subject_list. Regression command: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py -k empty_subject_list
close_reason: Fixed StudyDataset.get_subject_data([]) by returning an empty data matrix via subject-backend get_data before concatenation. Added regression test test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_empty_subject_list and verified with: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py -k "empty_subject_list"

title: Python fmri_series parity: add output='DelayedMatrix'
description: Implemented fmri_series output='DelayedMatrix' support + added regression tests for lazy and invalid output paths.
close_reason: Added fmri_series/output parity support for DelayedMatrix and invalid-output validation; added regression coverage and verified tests.

title: Fix latent backend get_dims open-state check
description: Parity mismatch with R: latent backend get_dims should fail after close (is_open false), but current Python implementation only checks _dims and can return stale dims when closed.
close_reason: Added open-state guard to get_dims and regression tests for pre-open and post-close access.

title: test-as_delarr.R omits loading package namespace
description: Fixed isolated execution of test-as_delayed_array.R by loading package namespace and invoking internal delayed-array registration via fmridataset:::register_delayed_array_support(). Regression: Rscript -e 'testthat::test_file(tests/testthat/test-as_delayed_array.R)' now passes (1 skip).
close_reason: Added namespace guard (library(fmridataset)) and export assertions so as_delarr tests run in testthat helper context.

title: Resolve floating-coordinate selector matrices in fmri_series
description: R allows numeric (floating) 3-column selector matrices via resolve_selector coordinate mode, but Python only accepted integer dtype, rejecting valid legacy arrays. Add float support while preserving out-of-mask filtering parity.
close_reason: Fixed resolve_selector to accept numeric 3-column selectors with float dtype by using float-index math, preserving masked-coordinate mapping behavior; added regression test test_array_coordinate_matrix_float and verified it passes.

title: Normalize default Python matrix_dataset event_table schema for parity with R
description: Python matrix_dataset default event_table was empty (0x0) causing rpy2 conversion/parity test failures. Added parity-safe schema default with event_index column to match R behavior; added regression in python/tests/test_dataset.py.
close_reason: Fix implemented and regression verified

title: Python matrix_dataset vector input should be column matrix
description: R matrix_dataset accepts 1D vectors as column matrices via as.matrix(). Python currently reshapes 1D arrays to 1xN rows using np.atleast_2d, causing run_length mismatches and incorrect orientation. Fix by reshaping 1D inputs to (-1,1) and adding regression coverage.
close_reason: Implemented vector input parity fix (reshape 1D to column matrix) and added tests in python/tests/test_dataset.py

title: latent_dataset.get_data should return scores like R; add reconstruct_voxels path in backend
description: R latent_dataset.get_data.latent_dataset warns and returns basis (latent scores), while Python currently returns reconstructed voxels via backend.get_data. Align APIs by making LatentBackend.get_data return latent scores, adding backend reconstruct_voxels(), and having LatentDataset get_data warn + forward to scores and reconstruct_voxels method use backend voxel reconstruction.
close_reason: Implemented parity fix: LatentBackend.get_data returns latent scores, LatentDataset.get_data now warns and delegates to scores, backend reconstruct_voxels introduced and used by LatentDataset, and full regression suite passes.

title: Latent backend component selection drops dimensions and skips index validation
description: LatentBackend.get_loadings(components) accepts scalar selectors as a 0-d array, returning a 1-D vector and breaking matrix parity; reconstruct_voxels() also lacked rows/voxels validation, allowing inconsistent error behavior versus other latent/matrix backends. Added validation via _validate_indices and 2-D-preserving component selection with regression tests.
close_reason: Applied _validate_indices in latent reconstruction/loadings paths and added regression tests for scalar component shape plus invalid reconstruction indices; full Python test suite passes.

title: Infer run_length in latent_dataset when omitted
description: R latent_dataset allows run_length=NULL/empty to infer per-file lengths from backend; Python currently requires run_length. Add inference fallback and update signature/tests.
close_reason: latent_dataset now allows optional run_length; infers per-source lengths from LatentBackend.run_lengths when omitted or zero, matching R behavior.

title: Add components filtering to python latent loadings
description: Parity gap: Python LatentDataset.get_spatial_loadings lacks components argument present in R
close_reason: Added components selector to LatentDataset.get_spatial_loadings with regression coverage.

title: StudyDataset.get_subject_data should support 1-based numeric subject_id
description: R get_subject_data/get_data_matrix accepts numeric subject_id as positional indices. Python currently only matched by exact subject_id values, so subject_ids like ['S01','S02'] with subject_id=1/2 failed. Fix by falling back to 1-based positional lookup after exact-id miss.
notes: Fix in python/src/fmridataset/study_dataset.py and add regression test test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_numeric_index. Command: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py -k "numeric_index"
close_reason: Patched get_subject_data to support R-like 1-based numeric indexing fallback when subject_id is not an exact ID match. Added regression test test_study_dataset_access.py::test_get_subject_data_numeric_index and verified with: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py -k "numeric_index"

title: Matrix dataset should reject non-2D datamat
description: Python matrix_dataset currently flattens arrays with ndim > 2 into a column vector. This diverges from R behavior, which expects matrix-like input and fails on multi-dimensional structures. Added a 2-D validation error and updated regression test.
close_reason: Added 2-D-only validation in python/src/fmridataset/dataset_constructors.py and updated python/tests/test_dataset.py::test_multi_dimensional_datamat_rejected; regression verified with python -m pytest python/tests/test_dataset.py -k multi_dimensional_datamat_rejected and full python test set.

title: Ported api parity: study dataset and h5/new_fmri_series visibility regressions
description: fmridataset test suite shows stale S3 dispatch/visibility regressions:  on  returns NULL, required h5_backend method symbols  are not exported/visible, and  is not exported in test context.
notes: Fixed: added @export to new_fmri_series in R/FmriSeries.R; added regression in tests/testthat/test_api_safety.R asserting exported namespace includes %||%, new_fmri_series, and h5_backend methods backend_open/close/get_dims/get_mask/get_data/get_metadata. Validation: Rscript -e "devtools::load_all(quiet=TRUE); testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test_api_safety.R', reporter='summary')" => api_safety PASS.
close_reason: fixed by exporting new_fmri_series and adding namespace regression test; verified API safety test file passes

title: StudyBackend get_data should validate row/col indices and logical masks
description: Python StudyBackend accepted out-of-bounds and invalid indices via numpy's permissive indexing, unlike R which validates rows/cols in backend_get_data.study_backend. Also lacked logical mask support. Added validation helper and regressions in python/tests/test_study_backend.py.
close_reason: Implemented StudyBackend.get_data index validation (bounds, integer, and bool-mask logical selectors) and added regressions; pytest -q python/tests/test_study_backend.py passes (21 passed).

title: Align default event_table parity for backend-backed fmri and latent datasets
description: Python FmriDataset currently adds an  column for any zero-column event_table, so fmri_dataset()/latent_dataset() return 0x1 tables. R constructors keep default event_table as true empty 0x0. Move event_table normalization to matrix_dataset only to restore constructor parity and add regressions for fmri/latent defaults.
close_reason: Restored parity by removing global zero-column event_table normalization from FmriDataset.__init__ and added regressions confirming fmri_dataset()/latent_dataset() default event_table remains empty (0x0) like R.

title: Avoid zero-column event_table in matrix_dataset for Python parity
description: Added matrix_dataset default event_table normalization to avoid zero-column tables and added regression test ensuring non-empty schema by default in test_api_safety.R. Verified with parity and API tests.

title: Resolve base_path in latent_dataset source paths
description: R latent_dataset applies base_path to relative source files; Python currently passes source paths directly so  fails unless cwd matches. Add base_path resolution and regression.
close_reason: latent_dataset now resolves relative source paths against base_path (like R) and regression covers creating dataset from relative.lv.h5 with explicit base_path.

title: StudyBackend get_data should validate row/col indices
description: Python StudyBackend.get_data currently accepted negative/out-of-range/float indices without validation, leading to silent wraparound/invalid selection unlike R and other backends. Add integer+bounds validation and regression tests.
close_reason: Added missing bounds/integer validation in StudyBackend via _validate_indices and added regressions for negative/out-of-range/float indices in python/tests/test_study_dataset_access.py; full python tests pass.

title: Clarify fmri_dataset deprecated backend argument contract
description: Regression in tests/testthat/test_additional_cases.R: deprecated backend argument now requires storage backend object; verified with devtools::load_all and test_file.
close_reason: Source already has explicit deprecated backend guard and message; validated by devtools::load_all; test_file(test_additional_cases.R) passes. Test checks fmri_dataset(..., backend="nifti") errors with explicit storage-backend-object contract.

title: Reject character run_length in python constructors
description: R dataset constructors reject character run_length (e.g., "2") with invalid type errors, but Python currently accepted it via float coercion. Added shared-run_length guard in _coerce_run_length and regression tests in python/tests/test_dataset.py for matrix_dataset and fmri_dataset.
notes: Fix in _coerce_run_length: reject (str, bytes)-like run_length values before float conversion; regression tests added in python/tests/test_dataset.py for matrix_dataset and fmri_dataset.

title: Align python latent_dataset get_mask with R component mask and add regression
close_reason: Closed

title: test_as_delarr.R requires package namespace load
description: tests/testthat/test_as_delarr.R runs functions from fmridataset without attaching the package namespace and can fail when executed standalone; add library(fmridataset) + regression assertions.
close_reason: Added library(fmridataset) and export availability check to tests/testthat/test_as_delarr.R; verified via Rscript testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test_as_delarr.R').

title: Infer latent run_length for 0/empty aliases
description: Python latent_dataset should mirror R behavior where run_length=NULL/0/empty triggers backend-per-file run-length inference instead of strict validation.
close_reason: Added latent run_length inference when None/0/empty, added tests for omitted/0/empty multi-source, and guarded run-length cache reset on reopen in LatentBackend.

title: Enforce unique event_table columns in Python fmri_dataset and latent_dataset
description: Python fmri_dataset/latent_dataset accepted duplicate event_table column names despite R using tibble::as_tibble(..., .name_repair='check_unique'). Add validation and regression tests.
close_reason: Added _ensure_event_table_unique in Python constructors, applied to fmri_dataset + latent_dataset, and added regression tests in python/tests/test_dataset.py and python/tests/test_latent_dataset.py.

title: rpy2 parity tests use deprecated numpy2ri.activate
close_reason: Parity tests no longer use deprecated numpy2ri.activate; test_parity_dataset.py explicitly patches and asserts neither activate nor deactivate are called.

title: StudyDataset.get_subject_data should accept numpy subject ID arrays
description: Python study_dataset.get_subject_data currently treats 1-D numpy arrays as scalars, so array subject_id inputs with multiple IDs fail. This causes parity drift from R study dataset APIs that accept vector subject IDs.
notes: Fix: allow numpy arrays of subject IDs in StudyDataset.get_subject_data by flattening numpy arrays before subject lookup. Regression: test_get_subject_data_accepts_numpy_subject_id_array now verifies np.array([S01, S02]) returns both subject blocks. Test command: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset.py -k "get_subject_data_accepts_numpy_subject_id_array"
close_reason: Fixed study_dataset.get_subject_data to support numpy array subject IDs (flatten arrays before lookup), added regression test in python/tests/test_study_dataset.py, and validated with pytest command: PYTHONPATH=python/src python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_study_dataset.py -k "get_subject_data_accepts_numpy_subject_id_array"

title: Export missing study/n_runs and backend constructors in fmridataset
description: Fix regression: fmri_study_dataset n_runs and exported symbols required by tests.
close_reason: Fixed n_runs study dataset dispatch, exported missing backend symbols, and exposed new_fmri_series; targeted regressions pass via devtools load_all tests.

title: Expose FmriDataset.get_dims proxy
description: Matrix and voxel selectors call dataset.get_dims(), but FmriDataset only exposes backend.get_dims(). Add a compatibility proxy method so selector helpers work consistently across all dataset types.
close_reason: Added FmriDataset.get_dims() delegating to backend plus regression coverage in python/tests/test_dataset.py::TestDataAccess::test_get_dims so selectors and callers can use dataset-level dimension access.

title: fmridataset parity scan continuation
description: placeholder
close_reason: Created by mistake; superseded by fmridataset-8lg.

title: Support censor vector in python latent_dataset
description: R latent_dataset accepts optional censor but Python latent_dataset constructor always defaults to zeros and cannot pass custom censor
close_reason: Implemented Python latent_dataset censor argument propagation to FmriDataset with regressions verifying custom and default censor behavior.
